title: AdministrativeUnit
description: A single node in Uganda's administrative hierarchy (region, district, city, county, municipality, subcounty, town council, division, parish, ward, village, cell, or zone).

id.description: Stable identifier, formatted as 'level:slug' (e.g. 'district:kampala'). Safe to persist as a foreign key in external systems.

aliases.description: Alternate spellings or former names (e.g. 'Ssembabule' for 'Sembabule').

level.description: Which tier of the hierarchy this record occupies.

type.description: Sub-classification within a level, e.g. a 'district'-level record may have type 'district' or 'capital_city' (Kampala — note this describes the unit itself, not its governing authority; KCCA is the authority that administers the capital_city unit, analogous to how a District Local Government administers an ordinary district); a 'county'-level record may have type 'county' or 'municipality'.

parent_id.description: id of the containing unit one level up. Null only for level=region.

region_id.description: Denormalized pointer to the top-level region, for fast filtering regardless of depth.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Only 'operational' units should be treated as ground truth by consuming applications by default.

confidence.description: verified = cross-checked against 2+ current independent sources. legacy = imported from a dated source and not yet reconciled to the current structure. unverified = present but not yet source-checked at all.

effective_date.description: Date the unit became operational, if known.

external_refs.description: Arbitrary identifiers/codes carried over from a source dataset (e.g. a legacy numeric id or code), preserved for traceability and backward compatibility with older integrations.
